quality.rationale: 度规行列式与雅可比矩阵关联推导+全反对称张量唯一性+四维度规背景下的广义协变性计算

origin.question: Problem 3.20. Prove that, except for scaling by a constant, there is a unique tensor $\varepsilon_{\alpha \beta \gamma \delta}$ which is totally antisymmetric on all its 4 indices. The usual choice is to take $\varepsilon_{0123} = 1$ in Minkowski coordinates. What are the components of $\varepsilon$ in a general coordinate frame, with metric $g_{\mu \nu}$ ?
origin.solution:
Solution 3.20. If $\varepsilon_{\alpha  eta \gamma \delta}$ is totally antisymmetric, then given any one component e.g. $\varepsilon_{0123}$ any other component (with nonrepeated indices) can be found by permuting the indices on that given component. Components with repeated indices, of course, vanish. The tensor is thus fixed once $\varepsilon_{0123}$ is given, i.e. the tensor is unique up to scaling by a constant. With the usual, position independent choice of normalization, we have
$$
\varepsilon_{0 1 2 3} = - \varepsilon_{1 0 2 3} = \varepsilon_{1 0 3 2} = \dot s = 1
$$
in Minkowski coordinates. If we now transform to other coordinates $\mathbf{x}^{\mu^{\prime}}(\mathbf{x}^{\alpha})$ we get
$$
\varepsilon_{\mu^{
u \lambda^{\prime} \sigma^{\prime}}} =  rac {\partial \mathbf {x}^{a}}{\partial \mathbf {x}^{\mu^{\prime}}}  rac {\partial \mathbf {x}^{ eta}}{\partial \mathbf {x}^{
u^{\prime}}}  rac {\partial \mathbf {x}^{\gamma}}{\partial \mathbf {x}^{\lambda^{\prime}}}  rac {\partial \mathbf {x}^{\delta}}{\partial \mathbf {x}^{\sigma^{\prime}}} \varepsilon_{a}  eta \gamma \delta = \det \left[  rac {\partial \mathbf {x}^{a}}{\partial \mathbf {x}^{a^{\prime}}}ight] \varepsilon_{\mu 
u \lambda \sigma}.
$$
Now
$$
\mathrm {g}_{\mu^{\prime} 
u^{\prime}} =  rac {\partial_{\mathbf {x}^{\alpha}}}{\partial_{\mathbf {x}^{\mu^{\prime}}}}  rac {\partial_{\mathbf {x}^{ eta}}}{\partial_{\mathbf {x}^{
u^{\prime}}}} \eta_{\alpha}  eta
$$
$$
\det \left(\mathrm {g}_{\mu^{\prime} 
u^{\prime}}ight) = \left| \det \left( rac {\partial \mathrm {x}^{\alpha}}{\partial \mathrm {x}^{\alpha^{\prime}}}ight)ight|^{2} \det (\eta_{\alpha  eta})
$$
so that
$$
\det \left(\partial \mathbf {x}^{a} / \partial \mathbf {x}^{a^{\prime}}ight) = \left[ - \det \left(\mathbf {g}_{\mu^{\prime} 
u^{\prime}}ight)ight]^{ rac {1}{2}}
$$
and finally
$$
\varepsilon_ { \mu \acute {
u} ^ { \prime } \lambda^ { \prime } \sigma^ { \prime } } = \left[ - \det \left(\mathbf {g} _ {u ^ { \prime } 
u}ight) ight] ^ {  rac {1}{2} } \varepsilon_ { \mu 
u \lambda \sigma }.
